title: valeriana excelsa subsp. procurrens
intro: Valeriana excelsa subsp. procurrens, also known as the large valerian, is a species of flowering plant in the family Valerianaceae. It is native to the Andes mountains in South America and is a type of valerian plant used for its medicinal and therapeutic properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat anxiety, insomnia, restlessness, and other sleep disorders due to its sedative and calming effects. Valeriana excelsa subsp. procurrens is often prepared in herbal teas, tinctures, and capsules, which can be used to promote relaxation, reduce stress, and improve overall sleep quality.
